baseline_full_repository_run.publication_failure_scope: Git publication transport only; all preceding validation steps completed successfully.
passed_steps: Rebuild final evidence and verify generated state

compatibility_scope: Recorded cross-platform baseline from the preceding complete repository validation; not a claim that the current main tree was rerun on every platform.

focused_current_change_regression.environment: Isolated Python 3.13 sandbox with the changed runtime and report modules copied exactly from the main-branch edits.

interpretation: This is composite validation evidence, not a current-tree digest receipt.
The recorded GitHub Actions run completed all scientific and engineering validation steps but failed only while publishing its generated commit after the branch moved.
The current scientific-quality and visual-report changes passed 14 focused regression tests in an isolated local environment.
The connector-created main-branch pushes did not produce a new observable GitHub Actions run, so this file does not claim current-tree end-to-end CI or fresh cross-platform execution.
Software validation does not imply scientific acceptance of external calculations, experiments, medical claims, legal conclusions or safety decisions.
limitations: No current-main source-tree digest is asserted in composite mode.
The current main tree was not rerun end-to-end on GitHub Actions during this execution because connector-created pushes did not emit an observable workflow event.
Cross-platform statuses are retained only as the last recorded baseline.
External literature retrieval, DFT, MD, FEM, CFD, process simulation, instrument operation and laboratory work require separate execution receipts.

workflow.name: Composite evidence: full repository baseline plus focused current-change regression
